title: Haramein 2008 Table 1 PASS: fixed-d hyperbola + phi^2 integer-quadratic-form ratios
_fixture_purpose: Proves that the six canonical scale-points of Haramein/Rauscher/Hyson 2008 Table 1 (Big Bang/Planck, Atomic, Stellar Solar, Galactic G1, Galactic G2, Universe), treated as integer (log10 R cm, log10 nu Hz) tuples, lie on a QA fixed-d hyperbola (d = b + e = const, the Schwarzschild line R*nu = c to within +-1 decade of rounding), and exhibit four structural segment-ratios on 2D Euclidean distances in (log R, log nu) whose integer quadratic-form quotients approximate phi^2 or 1/phi^2 to within 7%. Null probability < 5e-6 against uniform slope-minus-1 line placements (200000 samples). This places Haramein's hierarchy in the Q(sqrt 5) = Z[phi] algebraic family on a fixed-d QA diagonal, distinct from [217] Fuller b=e D_1 class.
source_attribution: Will Dale + Claude 2026-04-13. Primary source: Haramein, N.; Rauscher, E.A.; Hyson, M. (2008). Scale Unification — A Universal Scaling Law for Organized Matter. In Cs Varga, I. Dienes, R.L. Amoroso (eds.), Proc. Unified Theories Conference, Budapest. PDF acquired 2026-04-13 from spacefed.com and cached at Documents/haramein_rsf/scale_unification_2008.pdf. Table 1 values OCR'd from PDF page 5 and cross-verified against body-text derivations for Universe (page 2, R_u ~ 10^28 cm, nu_u ~ 10^-17 Hz) and Schwarzschild-proton (pages 6-7, T = 5.537e-24 s, nu = 1.806e22 Hz). Theory note: docs/theory/QA_HARAMEIN_SCALING_DIAGONAL.md.

structural_claim.scaling_law: log10 R(cm) + log10 nu(Hz) = log10 c(cm/s) ~= 10.48; decade-rounded to d = b + e ~= 10 in {9, 10, 11}
structural_claim.diagonal_class: fixed-d hyperbola (b + e = const); distinct from [217] b=e D_1 diagonal and from [219] Fibonacci-order-1 structure
structural_claim.phi_structure: four 2D-Euclidean segment-ratios approximate {phi^2, 1/phi^2} on integer quadratic forms over (delta b, delta e)
structural_claim.algebraic_family: Q(sqrt 5) = Z[phi]; companion to [219] Fibonacci Resonance on different diagonal class

null_test.procedure: N random 6-point draws on slope-minus-1 line within box (log R in [-33, 28], intercept jittered in [9, 11]); compute the same four structural segment-ratios at the same sorted-index positions and sum |log(ratio/target)| across the four ratios.
null_test.script_note: reproduced inline in docs/theory/QA_HARAMEIN_SCALING_DIAGONAL.md Section 4; exact reproduction requires numpy with default_rng(0)

witnesses.description: All 6 Table-1 rows have b + e in {9, 10, 11} — a fixed-d hyperbola to within +-1 decade of rounding.
witnesses.verification: R*nu = c (Schwarzschild line); decade-rounded integer exponents sum to log10(c) ~= 10.48 rounded to nearest decade.

witnesses.description: Ratio BB-A / G2-U = 1250/481 ~= 2.599, which is phi^2 = 2.618 within 0.7%. Computed from integer deltas: (25^2 + 25^2) / (16^2 + 15^2) = 1250 / 481.
200000 random 6-point slope-minus-1 placements never produced a test statistic below the observed 0.077. Null 5th percentile was 1.584 (~20x observed).
witnesses.verification: p < 5e-6 gives structural significance; Haramein's phi-spacing is not an artifact of 6 random points on a slope-minus-1 line.

related_certs: [219] QA Fibonacci Resonance (same Q(sqrt 5) algebraic family, different domain)
[217] QA Fuller VE Diagonal Decomposition (different diagonal class: b=e D_1)
[191] QA Bateson Learning Levels (scale hierarchy methodology)
